perturbation.original_text: 1.26 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means: (a) with respect to the efforts to be expended, or considerations to be undertaken, by a Party or its Affiliate with respect to any objective, activity or decision to be undertaken hereunder, reasonable, good faith efforts to accomplish such objective, activity or decision as such Party would normally use to accomplish a similar objective, activity or decision under similar circumstances; and (b) with respect to Development and Commercialization of any Licensed Product for any indication by a Party, efforts and resources consistent with those efforts and resources commonly used by a similarly situated biotechnology company with respect to a product owned by it or to which it has similar rights, which product is at a similar stage in its development or product life and is of similar market potential taking into account (i) the patent and other proprietary position of the Licensed Product and (ii) the anticipated profitability of the Licensed Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.26 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means: (a) with respect to the efforts to be expended, or considerations to be undertaken, by a Party or its Affiliate with respect to any objective, activity or decision to be undertaken hereunder, any effort a Party deems reasonable, regardless of good faith, to accomplish such objective, activity or decision; and (b) with respect to Development and Commercialization of any Licensed Product for any indication by a Party, minimal efforts and resources, not necessarily consistent with those efforts and resources commonly used by a similarly situated biotechnology company, without consideration of the patent and proprietary position of the Licensed Product and its anticipated profitability.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts.' The original definition specifies 'reasonable, good faith efforts' and considers industry standards and profitability. The modified definition removes the 'good faith' requirement and minimizes the efforts needed, disregarding industry standards and profitability. This in-text contradiction contradicts the intent of Section 4.1 where CytoDyn shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to execute and perform the activities set forth in the Development Plan, creating ambiguity around the scope of the agreed development activities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Development Plan and Development Activities. CytoDyn shall have sole responsibility for, and final decision-making authority with respect to, performance of Development of the Licensed Product for the Initial Indication and any Subsequent Indication. CytoDyn shall use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to execute and perform the activities set forth in the Development Plan. CytoDyn shall conduct the activities under the Development Plan, and shall ensure that its Affiliates and contractors conduct their activities under the Development Plan, in a good scientific manner and in material compliance with Applicable Law, including cGLP, cGCP, cGMP and applicable national and international guidelines. For clarity, the Development Plan will only include activities related to indications in the Field.
perturbation.justification: The modification of the definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' introduces a clear and direct contradiction. The original definition mandates 'reasonable, good faith efforts' and consideration of industry standards and profitability, whereas the modified definition removes the 'good faith' requirement, allows for minimal effort, and disregards industry standards and profitability. This directly contradicts Section 4.1, which requires CytoDyn to 'use Commercially Reasonable Efforts to execute and perform the activities set forth in the Development Plan.' The modified definition significantly alters the scope and intent of this obligation, making it ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the level of effort CytoDyn is required to exert in the Development Plan.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Commercialization Plan. Attached as Attachment C is a written commercialization plan setting forth anticipated material Commercialization activities to be performed for the Licensed Product in the Initial Indication in the Territory (the "Commercialization Plan"). Vyera shall conduct the Commercialization activities in accordance with the Commercialization Plan and in performing such activities will ensure that it meets or exceeds the Minimum Requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Commercialization Plan. Attached as Attachment C is a written commercialization plan setting forth anticipated material Commercialization activities to be performed for the Licensed Product in the Initial Indication in the Territory (the "Commercialization Plan"). Vyera may conduct the Commercialization activities in accordance with the Commercialization Plan, but in performing such activities there's no obligation to meet or exceed the Minimum Requirements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that Vyera meet or exceed the Minimum Requirements outlined in the Commercialization Plan. The changed text removes this obligation, creating a contradiction. Another instance of contradiction would be in Section 5.5 (a) the Minimum Requirements may not be modified, amended or otherwise changed without the written consent of CytoDyn, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed; creating a contradiction on the adherence of the Commercialization plan.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5 Decisions that are not Reserved. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Article 5 or any other section of this Agreement: (a) the Minimum Requirements may not be modified, amended or otherwise changed without the written consent of CytoDyn, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed; and [***].
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 5.2 changes 'shall conduct' to 'may conduct' and removes the obligation to meet or exceed minimum requirements. This directly contradicts Section 5.5(a), which states that the Minimum Requirements may not be modified or changed without written consent. By removing the obligation to meet these requirements, Section 5.2 effectively modifies the Minimum Requirements without consent, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.6 Royalty Obligation. Vyera shall pay to CytoDyn royalties equal to fifty percent (50%) of Net Sales of Licensed Products in the Territory during the Royalty Term; provided that, after the Step-Down Date, the royalty percentage will be reduced to [***] of Net Sales of Licensed Products in the Territory throughout the remaining period in the Royalty Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.6 Royalty Obligation. Vyera shall pay to CytoDyn royalties equal to fifty percent (50%) of Net Sales of Licensed Products in the Territory if, and only if, Vyera in its sole discretion determines that the Net Sales of Licensed Products in the Territory are sufficient to warrant such royalty payments; provided that, after the Step-Down Date, the royalty percentage may, or may not, be reduced, dependent of Vyera's considerations. If Step-Down Date is achived that should be based on Vyera's final decision.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes a clear royalty obligation based on Net Sales. The modified text makes the royalty payment contingent on Vyera's subjective determination of sufficiency. This also creates a contradiction with Section 8.8, which outlines how royalty payments are calculated and reported, creating uncertainty about how royalties are to be calculated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.8 Royalty Report; Payment. Within forty-five (45) days following the end of each Calendar Quarter after the First Commercial Sale of each Licensed Product in the Territory, Vyera shall provide CytoDyn with a report in a form reasonably acceptable to CytoDyn containing the following information for the applicable Calendar Quarter for such Licensed Product: (a) the amount of gross sales of the Licensed Product in the Territory; (b) an itemized calculation of Net Sales in the Territory showing reasonably detailed deductions; provided for in the definition of "Net Sales"; (c) a reasonably detailed calculation of the royalty payment due on such sales; (d) an accounting of the number of units of the Licensed Product sold; and (e) the application of the reduction, if any, made in accordance with the terms of Section 8.7. Vyera shall pay all amounts due to CytoDyn with respect to Net Sales by Vyera or its Affiliates for such Calendar Quarter at the time of the submission of such quarterly report.
perturbation.justification: The original text (8.6) clearly outlines a royalty obligation based on Net Sales. The modified text introduces Vyera's discretion in determining whether royalty payments are warranted, directly conflicting with the clear calculation and reporting requirements in section 8.8. Section 8.8 requires 'a reasonably detailed calculation of the royalty payment due on such sales', while the modified 8.6 makes the payment conditional on Vyera's discretion. This inconsistency creates a significant conflict regarding the obligation to pay royalties, rendering Section 8.8 unenforceable as it is premised on an obligation that is now subject to Vyera's subjective determination. This qualifies as an ambiguity leading to an in-text contradiction.
